description: Mostly seen in company of Forster's Tern. Wings nearly uniform pale above except dark 'carpal bar' and narrow dark trailing edge to outer 2/3 or so of primaries. No dark on secondaries. No dark elsewhere on on primaries. Underwings white with narrow blackish trailing edge on outermost two-thirds or so of primaries. Body white. Head white with black "tonsure" typical of immature (and basic plumage) Arctic and Common Terns. Bill small and black. In flight, head looked stubby, flight less direct and "bouncier" than that of Forster's Tern. Perched, legs appeared short. Tail white and forked, appropriate length for immature bird, perhaps a bit longer than I'd expect in immature Common Tern. Carpal bar, perched and in flight, not quite as dark as that of COTE.
